01_overview.foreign_policy_doctrine: Multi-alignment, Strategic Autonomy, and Vasudhaiva Kutumbakam (World is One Family)
key_principles: Strategic autonomy - independent decision-making without bloc politics
Non-alignment 2.0 - engaging all powers while maintaining independence
Neighborhood First policy - prioritizing South Asian relations
Act East Policy - deep engagement with Southeast and East Asia
Link West Policy - comprehensive engagement with West Asia and Gulf
Connect Central Asia Policy - reviving historical Silk Road ties
SAGAR (Security and Growth for All in the Region) - Indian Ocean centrality
Panchamrit commitment - climate action and renewable energy leadership
Global South champion - voice for developing nations
Reformed multilateralism - advocating UN Security Council expansion
strategic_objectives: Position as net security provider in Indo-Pacific
Counter China's Belt and Road Initiative (BRI)
Resolve border disputes with China and Pakistan
Expand defense exports to $5 billion by 2025
Lead Global South on climate, trade, and development
Strengthen Quad as democratic alternative in Indo-Pacific
Diversify energy imports, achieve 50% renewable by 2030
Expand cultural soft power through yoga, Ayurveda, cinema
01_overview.global_positioning: Rising multi-aligned power bridging Global South and developed democracies, 5th largest economy (GDP), 3rd largest military (active personnel), nuclear weapons state, space power, and aspiring permanent UNSC member
01_overview.historical_context: Non-Aligned Movement founder (1961), Gandhi-Nehru idealism, post-1991 economic liberalization shift, 1998 nuclear tests, 2008 Indo-US civil nuclear deal, 2014 onwards 'Modi Doctrine' of proactive engagement

Ceasefire violations - 5,133 in 2020, reduced to near-zero after Feb 2021 DGMOs agreement
Nuclear flashpoint - Balakot airstrike (Feb 2019), Pulwama attack (2019), Uri attack (2016)
Pakistan's CPEC participation - India opposes as it passes through PoK
cooperation_areas: Kartarpur Corridor - visa-free access to Gurdwara Darbar Sahib (opened Nov 2019, 5,000 pilgrims/day capacity)
Indus Water Treaty - continues despite tensions, administered by Indus Water Commission
SAARC membership - both founding members (1985)
Cricket diplomacy - sporadic bilateral series, IPL player bans
Trade potential - estimated $37 billion if MFN status granted and NTBs removed (World Bank 2018)

countries.name: China

key_issues: Border dispute - 1962 war legacy, no agreed LAC delineation, 21 rounds of talks (1981-2020) failed
Galwan Valley clash - June 15, 2020: 20 Indian soldiers killed, 43 Chinese casualties (unconfirmed), first fatalities since 1975
Military standoff - 50,000+ troops each deployed in Ladakh since May 2020, 19 disengagement points negotiated
Tibet issue - India hosts Dalai Lama (Dharamshala since 1959), 110,000 Tibetan refugees, China claims Arunachal as 'South Tibet'
Belt and Road Initiative - India only major economy to boycott BRI (since 2017) as CPEC violates sovereignty
String of Pearls - Chinese ports in Gwadar (Pak), Hambantota (SL), Kyaukpyu (Myanmar) encircle India
Brahmaputra river damming - China building 11 dams on Yarlung Tsangpo, water flow impact on Assam/Bangladesh
Masood Azhar UN listing - China blocked JeM chief's terrorist designation until 2019
NSG membership - China blocks India's Nuclear Suppliers Group entry despite US backing
Trade imbalance - India's deficit $85 billion (2023-24), 67% of electronics imports from China
cooperation_areas: BRICS membership - founding member (2009), hosts New Development Bank, coordinates on global governance
SCO membership - joined 2017, cooperation on counter-terrorism, regional security
RIC (Russia-India-China) trilateral - foreign minister-level dialogue
Climate cooperation - both in BASIC group (Brazil, South Africa, India, China), Paris Agreement coalition
Ayurveda-TCM collaboration - traditional medicine knowledge exchange since 2003 MoU
Kailash Mansarovar Yatra - China facilitates Hindu pilgrimage (suspended since COVID)

people_to_people.diaspora_size: 25,000 Indians in Maldives (teachers, doctors, workers - 25% of workforce)

people_to_people.tourism: 200,000 Indian tourists/year (2023) - largest source market, 15% share, $300M revenue
people_to_people.cultural_links: Islam-influenced shared heritage (via Malabar), Malayalam understood in southern atolls, Bollywood popular

03_extended_neighbourhood.definition: Countries beyond immediate borders that are strategically vital for India's security, economic, and cultural interests - comprising Southeast Asia (Act East), Central Asia (Connect Central Asia), and West Asia/Gulf (Link West)

southeast_asia.policy_framework: Act East Policy (upgraded from Look East 2014)
southeast_asia.strategic_importance: ASEAN centrality in Indo-Pacific, $130B trade, maritime connectivity, China balancing, civilizational links

countries.name: Vietnam
countries.strategic_importance: Closest strategic partner in SE Asia, shared China concerns, South China Sea coordination
key_areas: Defense: $500M credit line, BrahMos missile talks, Cam Ranh Bay access, joint exercises (VINBAX)
Oil exploration: ONGC Videsh in Vietnamese waters (Block 128), China objects
Capacity building: Training 3,000+ Vietnamese officers since 2000
Trade: $14.8B bilateral (2023-24)

countries.name: Indonesia
countries.strategic_importance: Largest ASEAN economy, Malacca Strait guardian, G20 partner, Andaman Sea connectivity
key_areas: Defense: CORPAT exercises (IND-INDO), Sabang port development discussions
Maritime: EEZ overlaps near Andaman, joint patrolling
Trade: $29.4B (2023-24), palm oil, coal imports
Diaspora: 100,000 Indian origin (Bali Hindu links)

countries.name: Singapore
countries.strategic_importance: Financial hub, defense cooperation model, smart city collaboration, Indian diaspora
key_areas: Defense: SIMBEX (oldest naval exercise since 1994), Bold Kurukshetra (army), air force training
Trade: $32B (2023-24), Singapore largest FDI source in India ($15.7B in 2023-24)
Finance: GIFT City-Singapore linkage, UPI-PayNow integration (Feb 2023)
Diaspora: 650,000 Indians (18% of Singapore population)

countries.name: Thailand
countries.strategic_importance: Land connectivity via Myanmar, Buddhist links, tourism, Mekong cooperation
key_areas: Connectivity: India-Myanmar-Thailand Trilateral Highway (1,360 km), Kaladan project
Defense: MAITREE (army), Indo-Thai CORPAT (navy)
Trade: $15.2B (2023-24), FTA under negotiation
Buddhist tourism: 100,000+ Thai pilgrims to India annually

countries.name: Malaysia
countries.strategic_importance: Malacca Strait access, palm oil supplier, Tamil diaspora, electronics trade
key_areas: Trade: $19.4B (2023-24), India 10th largest partner
Diaspora: 2 million Indian origin (7% population), Tamil cultural links
Defense: MILAN exercises, maritime cooperation
Kashmir issue: Mahathir's comments (2019) caused friction

countries.name: Philippines
countries.strategic_importance: South China Sea frontline, BrahMos customer, Indo-Pacific alignment
key_areas: Defense: BrahMos deal signed (Jan 2022, $375M, 3 batteries delivered 2024)
Maritime: South China Sea coordination, UNCLOS alignment
Trade: $2.5B (2023-24), growth potential
Convergence: China containment, rules-based order advocacy

countries.name: Cambodia
countries.strategic_importance: Mekong subregion, Angkor Wat heritage, China influence balancing
key_areas: Heritage: Archaeological Survey of India restored Angkor Wat (1986-93), ongoing Ta Prohm restoration
Development: $100M credit lines since 2002
China factor: Ream naval base concerns, India's limited influence

countries.name: Laos
countries.strategic_importance: Mekong-Ganga Cooperation, Buddhist links, China's BRI showcase
key_areas: Connectivity: Mekong-Ganga Cooperation member, India-Laos development partnership
Development: $100M credit, HRD training, ITEC scholars
China factor: Laos-China railway dominant, India marginal presence

countries.name: Brunei
countries.strategic_importance: Energy cooperation, South China Sea claimant, modest bilateral engagement
key_areas: Energy: ONGC-Petronas discussions, LNG potential
Defense: Naval ship visits, limited exercises
Trade: $200M (2023-24), minimal

countries.name: Timor-Leste
countries.strategic_importance: Newest SE Asian nation (2002), potential ASEAN member, maritime neighbor
key_areas: Development aid: $5M+ since independence, capacity building
Defense: Small training programs
ASEAN: India supports Timor-Leste's ASEAN membership bid

trade_statistics.fta_status: ASEAN-India FTA (AIFTA) since 2010, review ongoing for upgrade

central_asia.strategic_importance: Energy resources, counter-terrorism, Afghanistan stability, historical Silk Road, SCO platform
challenges: No direct land access (Pakistan blocks, Afghanistan unstable)
Chinese BRI dominance ($50B+ investments)
Russian traditional influence
Limited Indian trade ($4B vs China's $100B)

countries.name: Kazakhstan
countries.strategic_importance: Largest Central Asian state, uranium supplier (world's largest reserves), energy corridor
key_areas: Uranium: India imports 5,000 tonnes/year (40% of needs), Kazatomprom supplies
Energy: Oil transit via INSTC, TAPI pipeline discussions
Defense: Military training, joint exercises
Trade: $2.5B (2023-24), uranium, oil, wheat

countries.name: Uzbekistan
countries.strategic_importance: Most populous Central Asian state, Afghanistan neighbor, connectivity hub
key_areas: Connectivity: Termez-Kabul-Peshawar route potential (via Afghanistan)
Pharmaceuticals: Indian pharma exports growing, 25% market share
IT: Indian IT companies establishing presence
Trade: $750M (2023-24), $1B target by 2025

countries.name: Turkmenistan
countries.strategic_importance: Gas reserves (4th largest globally), TAPI pipeline source
key_areas: TAPI: Turkmenistan-Afghanistan-Pakistan-India pipeline (1,800 km, $10B, stalled)
Gas potential: 600 trillion cubic feet reserves, India needs diversification
Neutrality: Limits partnership depth

countries.name: Tajikistan
countries.strategic_importance: Only non-Turkic state, Afghan border (1,400 km), counter-terrorism
key_areas: Defense: India's first foreign military base (Ayni/Farkhor airbase, operationalized 2006)
Counter-terrorism: RATS cooperation (SCO), intel sharing on Afghan spillover
Development: $70M+ lines of credit, IT center, schools

countries.name: Kyrgyzstan
countries.strategic_importance: SCO member, Indian student hub (pre-2023), transit potential
key_areas: Education: 15,000 Indian medical students (peaked 2022), declined after regulations
Mountain warfare: Joint exercises potential, terrain similarities (Himalayan-Tien Shan)
Trade: $80M (2023-24), limited

summit_mechanism.name: India-Central Asia Summit
summit_mechanism.launched: January 2022 (virtual, PM Modi + 5 Central Asian leaders)

trade_statistics.main_products: Uranium, oil, cotton, dry fruits (imports); pharma, tea, machinery (exports)
trade_statistics.comparison: China trades $100B+ with Central Asia, Russia $30B

west_asia_gulf.policy_framework: Link West Policy / Think West Policy (2005 onwards, accelerated 2015)
west_asia_gulf.strategic_importance: Energy security (40% oil, 50% gas imports), 9 million diaspora, $50B remittances, maritime trade, counter-terrorism

countries.name: United Arab Emirates
countries.strategic_importance: 3rd largest trading partner, 3.5 million diaspora, gold hub, counter-terrorism alignment
key_areas: Trade: $85B (2023-24, 3rd largest partner after China, USA)
CEPA: Comprehensive Economic Partnership Agreement (May 2022) - zero duty on 90% goods
Diaspora: 3.5 million Indians (30% of UAE population), largest expat community
Remittances: $15B/year (world's highest bilateral remittance corridor)
Defense: Joint naval exercises, first India-UAE Desert Cyclone exercise (2024)
Energy: ADNOC strategic oil reserves in India (Mangalore, 5.86 million barrels)
I2U2: Quad-like grouping with Israel, USA for food/energy security

countries.name: Saudi Arabia
countries.strategic_importance: Top oil supplier, 2.5 million diaspora, holy sites, $100B investment pledge
key_areas: Energy: 18% of India's oil imports (largest supplier), Aramco-Reliance refinery deal ($15B proposed)
Trade: $52.7B (2023-24), 5th largest partner
Diaspora: 2.5 million Indians (largest expat community in KSA)
Remittances: $8B/year
Investment: $100B in India announced (2019, slow implementation)
Counter-terrorism: Intelligence sharing, Masood Azhar coordination
Haj pilgrimage: 200,000 Indian pilgrims/year (2nd largest Haj contingent)
countries.vision_2030: India partner in Saudi diversification, tech/healthcare collaboration

countries.name: Iran
countries.strategic_importance: Chabahar port (Afghanistan/Central Asia access), energy potential, civilizational ties
key_areas: Chabahar: India's $500M investment, 10-year operation contract (May 2024), sanctions waiver
Energy: Oil imports collapsed (from 500,000 bpd to near-zero under US sanctions)
INSTC: International North-South Transport Corridor - India-Iran-Russia multimodal link
Afghanistan: Common concern on Taliban, refugee coordination
Balancing: India maintains ties despite US pressure, independent policy

countries.name: Israel
countries.strategic_importance: Defense technology, agriculture tech, counter-terrorism, I2U2 partner
key_areas: Defense: $1B+/year arms trade (India largest Israeli arms buyer), Barak missiles, drones, Phalcon AWACS
Agriculture: Drip irrigation, desert farming tech, 30 Indo-Israeli Centres of Excellence
Water: Desalination tech, wastewater recycling expertise
Counter-terrorism: Entebbe model, special forces training, intel sharing
Gaza conflict (2023-24): India's balanced approach - humanitarian concern, terrorism condemnation
countries.palestinian_position: India supports two-state solution, recognized Palestine 1988, UNRWA donor

countries.name: Qatar
countries.strategic_importance: LNG supplier, 750,000 diaspora, FIFA 2022 infrastructure builders
key_areas: Energy: India's largest LNG supplier (12 MTPA long-term contract), Petronet-RasGas deal
Diaspora: 750,000 Indians (largest expat group in Qatar, 25% population)
Trade: $18.7B (2023-24), mostly LNG imports
Navy visit: Death row commutation (2024) for 8 Indian naval veterans

countries.name: Kuwait
countries.strategic_importance: Oil supplier, 1 million diaspora, Gulf War evacuation legacy
key_areas: Diaspora: 1 million Indians (largest expat community, 30% population)
Remittances: $4.5B/year
Energy: 10% of India's crude oil imports
History: Air India's largest evacuation (170,000 Indians in 1990 Gulf War)

countries.name: Oman
countries.strategic_importance: Duqm port access, maritime cooperation, diaspora, Hormuz Strait proximity
key_areas: Duqm Port: India granted access (2018), $1.8B investment in special economic zone
Defense: IMNEX naval exercises, air force drills, Indian Navy logistics access
Diaspora: 700,000 Indians (15% of Oman population)
Trade: $12B (2023-24), oil, fertilizers

countries.name: Bahrain
countries.strategic_importance: US Fifth Fleet base, 350,000 diaspora, GCC gateway
key_areas: Diaspora: 350,000 Indians (largest expat community, 25% population)
Trade: $1.5B (2023-24), aluminum, oil
Naval visits: Indian Navy regular port calls
RuPay: Launched in Bahrain 2023

countries.name: Yemen
countries.strategic_importance: Bab-el-Mandeb Strait, Houthi piracy threat, evacuation preparedness
key_areas: Conflict: Civil war since 2014, India evacuated 5,600 Indians (Operation Rahat 2015)
Maritime: Houthi Red Sea attacks 2023-24, Indian Navy deployed INS Kolkata
Trade: Collapsed (<$100M), historically $2B+
UN: India supports peace process, humanitarian access

countries.name: Iraq
countries.strategic_importance: 3rd largest oil supplier, 20,000 workers, reconstruction opportunities
key_areas: Energy: 5th largest source of crude (1 million bpd), ONGC interest in oil blocks
Diaspora: 20,000 Indians (nurses, workers in Kurdistan)
Reconstruction: India offers expertise (infrastructure, pharma, IT)
Trade: $37.5B (2023-24), 90% oil imports

countries.name: Jordan
countries.strategic_importance: West Bank neighbor, Hashemite heritage, phosphate supplier
key_areas: Phosphate: India largest importer (5M tonnes/year for fertilizers)
Defense: Growing cooperation, training
Culture: Bollywood popular, Jordan tourism promotion

countries.name: Egypt
countries.strategic_importance: Suez Canal (60% of India-Europe trade), Arab world leader, NAM co-founder
key_areas: Maritime: Suez Canal transit vital (60% of India-Europe trade), Houthi disruptions concern
NAM legacy: Nehru-Nasser friendship, non-alignment co-founders
Defense: Brahmos discussions, joint exercises
Wheat: India exported 1.8M tonnes (2022) during shortage

gcc_engagement.oil_dependency: 40% of India's crude imports from GCC

oic_engagement.future: Observer status aspiration, but Pakistan blocks

USA.name: United States of America
historical_context: Cold War estrangement (1950s-1990s): India-USSR alignment, US tilt to Pakistan
1998 sanctions: Nuclear tests led to technology denial
2000s turnaround: Next Steps in Strategic Partnership (2004), Civil Nuclear Deal (2008)
2014 onwards: Rapid acceleration under Modi-Obama, Modi-Trump, Modi-Biden

frameworks: BECA (2020): Basic Exchange and Cooperation Agreement (geospatial intelligence)
COMCASA (2018): Communications Compatibility and Security Agreement
LEMOA (2016): Logistics Exchange Memorandum of Agreement

major_deals: GE-414 jet engine deal (2024, $1B+ for Tejas Mk2)

quad_membership.focus: Indo-Pacific security, vaccines, tech, climate, maritime
quad_membership.significance: India's first security grouping with Western powers

economic_ties.trade_surplus: $26.2 billion (India's largest surplus with any country)
trade_issues: GSP withdrawal (2019): $6.3B exports lost preferential access

institutional_mechanisms: iCET (Initiative on Critical and Emerging Technologies, 2023)

focus_areas: Defense tech: Co-production of jet engines
Space: Artemis Accords (India signed 2023), NISAR satellite
iCET_initiative.significance: First tech partnership at strategic level with any country

political_influence: Congressional Caucus on India (largest country-specific caucus, 100+ members)

recent_developments.event: PM Modi's 8th US visit - Quad Summit, Biden meeting, UNGA address
recent_developments.impact: iCET accelerated, defense deals finalized, $3B semiconductor investments announced

recent_developments.event: GE-414 engine deal signed - 80% tech transfer for Tejas Mk2

recent_developments.event: PM Modi's State Visit - addressed joint session of Congress (2nd time)
recent_developments.impact: Highest honors, MQ-9B drone deal announced, Comprehensive Global Strategic Partnership declared

RUS.name: Russian Federation
historical_context: USSR era (1947-1991): Closest ally, defense backbone, diplomatic support (UNSC vetoes)
1971 Treaty of Peace and Friendship: India-Soviet foundation during Bangladesh war
Post-Soviet transition: Brief uncertainty, 1993 Treaty restored ties
2014 onwards: Russia pivot to China creates challenges, but strategic autonomy maintained

major_deals: BrahMos JV (world's fastest cruise missile, $3B+ revenue)
joint_ventures: BrahMos Aerospace (50:50): 500+ missiles, export to Philippines, Vietnam

economic_ties.oil_imports: 40% of India's crude (up from 2% pre-2022), Russia now #1 supplier
economic_ties.rupee_trade: Rupee-Ruble mechanism expanded, but Ruble accumulation problem ($10B+ stuck)

ukraine_war_impact.india_position: Neutral - abstained on UN votes (13 times), called for dialogue
ukraine_war_impact.pm_modi_statement: This is not an era of war (Sept 2022 to Putin, Samarkand)
ukraine_war_impact.oil_purchase: Strategic acquisition of discounted crude ($30-40/barrel discount)
ukraine_war_impact.western_criticism: Muted due to India's strategic importance
ukraine_war_impact.balance: India successfully maintained ties with both West and Russia

recent_developments.event: PM Modi visits Moscow - first bilateral since Ukraine war, hugs Putin
recent_developments.impact: Western unease, but Modi raised prisoners/war concerns, reiterated dialogue position

recent_developments.event: INS Tushil (Krivak-class frigate) commissioned in Russia - last ship under current contract
recent_developments.impact: Defense partnership continues, but future contracts shifting to domestic production

recent_developments.event: Russia becomes India's #1 oil supplier - 40% share (up from 2%)
recent_developments.impact: Strategic energy security, $30-40B savings on discounted crude, rupee trade issues

JPN.name: Japan
historical_context: 2014: Special Strategic and Global Partnership (Abe-Modi era)

defense_cooperation.significance: Japan's first major defense export to non-US ally

key_projects: Mumbai-Ahmedabad High-Speed Rail ($15B, Shinkansen tech, 508 km)
economic_ties.oda_loans: $35 billion disbursed (1958-2024), India largest ODA recipient globally

connectivity_initiatives.asia_africa_growth_corridor: Japan-India alternative to BRI, focuses on Africa, quality infrastructure

recent_developments.event: Japan's PM Kishida visits India - semiconductor, hydrogen cooperation MoUs
recent_developments.impact: Supply chain resilience, $42B investment target by 2027, US-2 deal progressed

recent_developments.event: G7 Hiroshima Summit - PM Modi invited as guest, Global South voice
recent_developments.impact: Japan bridges India-West, affirms India's global role

FRA.name: France
historical_context: Post-1998 tests: France did not impose sanctions (unlike others)
First Strategic Partnership (1998): Model for India's future SPs
Nuclear cooperation: Civil nuclear deal (2008), submarines
Indo-Pacific partner: French territories (Réunion, New Caledonia) make it resident power

indo_pacific.france_territories: Réunion, Mayotte, New Caledonia, French Polynesia (1.6M French citizens in Indo-Pacific)
indo_pacific.significance: Only EU country with significant Indo-Pacific military presence

recent_developments.event: PM Modi chief guest at Bastille Day Parade - 1st world leader in 25 years
recent_developments.impact: Highest honor, defense deals accelerated, semiconductor cooperation MoU

recent_developments.event: India-France Horizon 2047 roadmap adopted
recent_developments.impact: 25-year vision, nuclear subs, jet engine, space collaboration

DEU.name: Germany
key_areas: Trade: $26 billion (2023-24), Germany is India's largest EU trade partner
Investment: $14 billion FDI cumulative, 1,700+ German companies
Green partnership: Hydrogen, renewable energy, €10B commitment by 2030
Skills: Dual education model adoption, 20,000 Indian students in Germany

recent_developments.event: Chancellor Scholz visits India - 6th IGC meeting, green hydrogen agreement
recent_developments.impact: €1.5B green transition support, skilled immigration pathways expanded

GBR.name: United Kingdom
key_areas: Trade: $20.4 billion (2023-24), FTA negotiations since 2022
Diaspora: 1.8 million British Indians (2.7% of UK population), politically influential
Education: 55,000 Indian students/year (2nd after China), record visas
Living Bridge: Largest source of legal immigrants to UK
Defense: Carrier Strike Group visit (2021), joint exercises
challenges: FTA stalled on immigration, tariffs
Colonial legacy discussions
Khalistan extremism in UK (India's concerns)

recent_developments.event: New Labour government (Starmer) - signals FTA completion priority
recent_developments.impact: Renewed momentum, investment protection issues remain

AUS.name: Australia
key_areas: Quad: Founding member, Indo-Pacific alignment
Trade: $26 billion (2023-24), ECTA FTA signed 2022 (zero duty on 96% goods)
Defense: 2+2 dialogue, AUSINDEX exercises, maritime cooperation
Resources: Coal (60% of India's imports), uranium (nuclear agreement 2014)
Diaspora: 800,000 Indian origin (largest non-European group), students 100,000+

ecta_impact.benefits: 96.4% Australian goods, 85% Indian goods at zero duty

recent_developments.event: PM Albanese visits India - cricket diplomacy at Ahmedabad Test
recent_developments.impact: Migration/Mobility partnership, space cooperation, defense education

KOR.name: Republic of Korea (South Korea)
key_areas: Trade: $24.6 billion (2023-24), CEPA since 2010
Investment: $6 billion FDI, Samsung, Hyundai, LG, Kia major investors
Defense: K9 Vajra howitzer ($1.86B, 100 units), shipbuilding cooperation
Technology: Semiconductors, 5G, electronics manufacturing
Culture: K-pop, Korean drama popular, soft power exchange

recent_developments.event: Samsung announces $4B additional investment in India (chip packaging)
recent_developments.impact: Supply chain integration, 'Trusted Korea' for electronics

SAARC.name: South Asian Association for Regional Cooperation
SAARC.india_role: Largest economy (70% of SAARC GDP), de facto leader, largest contributor to SAARC Development Fund ($100M)
key_achievements: SAARC University (established 2010, India hosts campus in New Delhi)
SAARC Satellite (GSAT-9, launched 2017, $235M gifted by India, Pakistan refused)
challenges: India-Pakistan hostility paralyzes decision-making
Summit stalled since 2014 (Kathmandu) - India boycotted 2016 Islamabad after Uri attack
Intra-SAARC trade only 5% of total (vs ASEAN's 25%)
China's observer status concern (2005)
Afghanistan's Taliban govt recognition issue
india_initiatives: SAARC Motor Vehicle Agreement (India pushed, Pakistan blocks)
Duty-free market access to SAARC LDCs (Bangladesh, Bhutan, Nepal, Maldives, Afghanistan)
Capacity building: 5,000+ scholarships/year to SAARC students
SAARC.alternative_platform: India now prioritizes BIMSTEC over SAARC (excludes Pakistan)
SAARC.exam_relevance: UPSC Prelims/Mains: Founding year, HQ, members, SAFTA, India's share, stalled summits

BIMSTEC.name: Bay of Bengal Initiative for Multi-Sectoral Technical and Economic Cooperation
BIMSTEC.significance: Bridge between South Asia and Southeast Asia, excludes Pakistan/Afghanistan (alternative to SAARC)
BIMSTEC.india_role: Lead coordinator for 4 sectors: Transport, Tourism, Environment, Counter-Terrorism
key_achievements: BIMSTEC FTA negotiations (pending since 2004, slow progress)

india_strategic_shift.2014_oath_ceremony: PM Modi invited BIMSTEC leaders (not SAARC) - signal of pivot
india_strategic_shift.rationale: Operationally effective vs SAARC's Pakistan blockage

connectivity_projects: BBIN Motor Vehicle Agreement (Bangladesh-Bhutan-India-Nepal, Myanmar to join)
challenges: Myanmar military coup (2021) complicates engagement
Slow FTA progress (20 years, no conclusion)
Limited intra-BIMSTEC trade (6% of total)
Funding issues (Secretariat budget constraints)
BIMSTEC.exam_relevance: UPSC: Founding year, members, sectors, India's lead areas, SAARC alternative

QUAD.name: Quadrilateral Security Dialogue
QUAD.membership_status: Founding Member (2007 informal, 2017 revival, 2021 Leaders' Summit)
QUAD.purpose: Free and Open Indo-Pacific, democratic values, maritime security, China balancing (unstated)
historical_evolution: 2007: Abe-Bush-Howard-Manmohan - first Quad dialogue (Chinese pressure killed it)
2021: First Quad Leaders' Summit (virtual, Biden initiative)
QUAD.india_role: Security provider in Indian Ocean, vaccine manufacturing hub, swing state balancing West-Russia
key_initiatives: Vaccine Partnership: Quad pledged 1 billion doses (India-manufactured, US-financed, Japan/Australia logistical support) - stalled due to Delta wave 2021
Infrastructure Coordination Group: Counter-BRI with quality infrastructure
Critical and Emerging Tech Working Group: AI, quantum, 5G/6G standards
Climate Working Group: Green shipping, hydrogen, renewables
Maritime Domain Awareness (IPMDA): Indo-Pacific surveillance sharing (launched May 2022)
Quad Fellowship: 100 STEM scholarships/year in US universities
exercises: Malabar Naval Exercise (all 4 since 2020, previously India-USA bilateral)
Annual air force exercises under discussion
india_red_lines: Strategic autonomy preservation (Russia ties, Iran oil)
QUAD.china_reaction: Labels Quad as 'Asian NATO', warns against bloc mentality
QUAD.exam_relevance: UPSC: Members, founding years, summits, vaccine partnership, IPMDA, Malabar, India's balancing

BRICS.name: Brazil, Russia, India, China, South Africa (expanded 2024: + Egypt, Ethiopia, Iran, UAE, Saudi Arabia)
BRICS.membership_status: Founding Member (2009 as BRIC, South Africa joined 2010)
BRICS.significance: 41% global population, 26% global GDP (PPP), 16% world trade, Global South voice
BRICS.india_role: Balancer between China's dominance and Global South aspirations, NDB headquarters alternate host
key_institutions: New Development Bank (NDB): HQ Shanghai, $100B capital, India 2nd largest shareholder (20%), President KV Kamath (2015-20)
Contingent Reserve Arrangement (CRA): $100B forex pool, alternative to IMF
india_achievements: NDB projects: $10B+ loans to India (renewable energy, infrastructure)
Counter-terrorism cooperation: India pushed BRICS Counter-Terrorism Strategy
Digital economy: BRICS Digital Economy Partnership Framework (India's initiative)
india_concerns: China dominance: 70% of BRICS GDP, NDB HQ in Shanghai
Expansion dilutes India's voice: New members (Iran, Egypt) closer to China/Russia
De-dollarization push: Common currency proposal (India cautious, prefers rupee trade)
Dual membership issue: BRICS vs Quad (India manages both)
BRICS.exam_relevance: UPSC: Founding year, members (original + new), NDB capital/HQ, CRA, summits hosted by India, expansion 2024

SCO.name: Shanghai Cooperation Organisation
SCO.purpose: Counter-terrorism, separatism, extremism (Three Evils), Central Asia stability, China-Russia led
SCO.india_role: Balancer, counter-terrorism focus, avoids military exercises with Pakistan, engages Central Asia
key_areas: RATS (Regional Anti-Terrorist Structure): Based in Tashkent, India participates in intel sharing
Economic cooperation: SCO Development Bank proposal (India cautious)
Connectivity: China's BRI vs India's non-participation
Afghanistan: Contact Group on Afghanistan (India active)
india_challenges: Pakistan presence: Tense bilaterals avoided, no joint exercises
China dominance: BRI framework dominates, India isolated
Limited economic gains: Intra-SCO trade $6T, India's share <5%
Military exercises: India skips Pakistan-involved drills
india_contributions: IT cooperation: Digital Stack sharing, UPI model promotion
Startups: SCO Startup Forum (India's initiative 2023 summit)
SCO.exam_relevance: UPSC: Founding year (2001), HQ, members, India's joining year (2017), RATS, Three Evils, observer/dialogue partners

I2U2.name: India-Israel-UAE-USA (I2U2)
I2U2.launch: July 2022 (Virtual Summit during Biden's West Asia visit)
I2U2.purpose: Food security, energy, water, space, health, transportation - practical cooperation
focus_sectors: Food Security: India's expertise + Israel's agritech + UAE's investment + US technology
Clean Energy: Solar, wind, hydrogen projects
Water: Desalination, wastewater recycling (Israel tech, Gulf needs, India market)
key_projects: India Integrated Food Parks: UAE investing $2B in India's food processing (Israel tech), announced at launch
Hybrid Renewable Energy Project: UAE financing, Israel tech, India deployment
Space collaboration: Earth observation for agriculture
I2U2.india_role: Largest market, food processor, tech hub, vaccine manufacturer
strategic_significance: Abraham Accords dividend: India benefits from Israel-UAE normalization

NSG.name: Nuclear Suppliers Group
NSG.status: Applicant (2008 exemption granted, membership blocked by China)
NSG.purpose: Control nuclear and dual-use exports to prevent proliferation
india_journey: 1974 Pokhran-I: NSG created in response (to stop India)
2008 exemption: US-India civil nuclear deal, NSG waiver granted (consensus)
2016 membership bid: Supported by 47/48 (China blocked citing NPT non-signatory status)
2024 status: Application pending, China insists on NPT accession (India refuses)
significance: Exemption allows: Uranium imports (Australia, Kazakhstan, Canada), reactor deals (US, France, Russia)
Membership would: Equal status, veto power on others' entry, legitimize nuclear status
China's hypocrisy: Gave similar exemption to Pakistan (undeclared)

impact: Import: Predator/Reaper drones from USA (30, $3.5B deal 2024)
